methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Tritone Technologies, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ofer Ben-Zur, is valued against a peer universe of 9 private and 1 public comparable. The private comp set includes stage-matched peers: 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M).

The peer-median method, applied across six same-sector, same-stage robotics companies, yields a median valuation of $1.1B. The peer range spans from $300M to $10.0B, with the tighter quartile-based analysis suggesting a defensible band of $914M (low) to $1.2B (high). This triangulation reflects the inherent dispersion within Series B robotics—a cohort marked by differentiated technical approaches and customer traction profiles.

The single-method framework limits conviction; a more granular view would benefit from revenue multiples, ARR growth rates, or customer concentration data not present in this dataset. The $1.1B median is notably sensitive to 1X Technologies' $10.0B valuation, which represents an outlier within the peer cohort. Absent additional methodological anchors, the analysis supports a valuation range of $914M to $1.2B, with $1.1B as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include Tritone's relative stage progression versus peers and unit economics relative to the median peer profile.
